skill.name: design-spec-extraction
skill.description: Extract structured design specifications from screenshots, Figma exports, UI mockups, and captured interfaces. The skill guides a seven-pass analysis that produces W3C DTCG-style design tokens, component trees, accessibility notes, and developer handoff data.
skill.summary: Extract design tokens, component structure, and accessibility notes from visual UI sources.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are false positives from Markdown code fences, JSON Schema keys, inline design token names, and prior audit text. The real behavior is a prompt-based design extraction workflow that writes local JSON files under .design-specs and suggests a benign mkdir command; no malicious intent, data exfiltration, credential access, or prompt-injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Local File Write Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill requires each extraction pass to write JSON outputs under .design-specs/{project}. This is expected for the skill, but users should understand that it creates or updates local project files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions repeatedly require writing named JSON files to a scoped .design-specs directory. This is a real filesystem behavior, but it is bounded and aligned with the skill purpose.

low_findings.title: Benign Directory Creation Command
low_findings.description: The skill suggests mkdir -p to create the .design-specs output directory. The command is hardcoded, scoped to a local project path, and does not include user-controlled shell interpolation beyond the project name placeholder.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of a shell command example, but it only creates a local output directory. No destructive command, network transfer, or credential access is present.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: Reported weak cryptography, Ruby backtick execution, screen capture upload, and network findings are not confirmed. The flagged text is Markdown formatting, JSON Schema metadata, design-token terminology, documentation URLs, or audit-report content rather than executable behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the high-volume matches come from Markdown fences, inline backtick formatting, $schema-style JSON keys, and descriptive text. No executable crypto, upload, or network call was found.

content.value_statement: Manual design handoff can miss tokens, spacing, and component details. This skill turns visual UI sources into structured specifications for implementation and review.
actual_capabilities: Guides a seven-pass extraction process for layout, colors, typography, components, spacing, states, and consolidation.
Produces local JSON outputs for each pass and a final consolidated design specification.
Maps visual styles into design token groups for colors, typography, spacing, sizing, shadows, and radii.
Builds a hierarchical component tree with bounds, component types, and style references.
Adds accessibility notes, including contrast checks, touch target observations, and semantic structure guidance.
Uses reference files for extraction patterns and a design-token schema for final output alignment.
limitations: Accuracy depends on the clarity, resolution, and completeness of the visual input.
Interactive states must be visible or provided as separate images for reliable extraction.
Measurements from screenshots are estimates unless source design data is available.
The skill writes local files and may suggest creating an output directory.

use_cases.title: Document a UI for Handoff
use_cases.description: Convert a finished mockup or screenshot into structured notes that developers can use during implementation.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap Design Tokens
use_cases.description: Extract color, type, spacing, and radius tokens from existing screens before formalizing a design system.

use_cases.title: Review Accessibility Details
use_cases.description: Identify contrast, touch target, and semantic structure issues while building the final design specification.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a Basic Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the design-spec-extraction skill on the attached screenshot. Create a project folder under .design-specs and produce the final design specification.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Design Tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Token-first extraction for a design system
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Figma export with design-spec-extraction. Prioritize color, typography, spacing, shadow, and radius tokens before consolidating the final spec.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Multiple States
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-spec-extraction on these default, hover, active, and disabled screenshots. Connect the states to the same component IDs in the final output.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Code Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full seven-pass design-spec-extraction workflow. Validate pass outputs, resolve token references, and include implementation notes for components and accessibility.

output_examples.input: A desktop landing page screenshot with hero, navigation, cards, and footer.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a final design summary with extracted color tokens, typography scale, spacing rules, component hierarchy, accessibility notes, and confidence scores.

output_examples.input: A Figma export of a dashboard with sidebar navigation and data cards.
output_examples.output: The skill creates pass files for layout, colors, type, components, spacing, states, and a consolidated handoff specification.

output_examples.input: Several button screenshots showing default, hover, active, and disabled states.
output_examples.output: The skill documents the component states, shared token references, visual differences, and accessibility considerations for implementation.

best_practices: Use high-resolution images and crop out browser chrome unless it is part of the design.
Provide separate screenshots for important responsive breakpoints and interactive states.
Review the generated pass files before using the final specification for implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use blurry or heavily compressed images when precise measurements matter.
Do not assume hidden interactions can be extracted from a single static screenshot.
Do not skip validation when the output will feed code generation or design system tooling.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Clear screenshots, Figma exports, mockups, and UI captures work best. Higher resolution improves token and measurement accuracy.

faq.question: Does it browse live websites?
faq.answer: No. The skill analyzes visual inputs that the user provides and writes local specification files.

faq.question: What files does it create?
faq.answer: It creates pass-by-pass JSON files and a final design-spec.json file under a .design-specs project directory.

faq.question: Can it detect accessibility issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides contrast, touch target, and semantic structure checks, but expert review is still recommended.

faq.question: Can I use the output for code generation?
faq.answer: Yes. The final structure is intended for design handoff and code generation workflows that consume design tokens and component specs.

faq.question: Will measurements be exact?
faq.answer: Measurements from screenshots are estimates. Source design exports usually provide better precision than compressed images.
